subject: Mathematics
title: Infinity in Computing
description: A micro-lesson that makes Infinity in Computing usable.

hook.text: Computers are finite machines. But they constantly wrestle with infinite concepts.

buildup.text: Floating-point numbers can't represent all reals. Computers approximate using finite binary digits.

discovery.text: An infinite loop is a program that runs forever—usually a bug, but sometimes intentional.

twist.text: Turing proved in 1936 that no program can decide whether an arbitrary program will halt or loop forever.

climax.text: The halting problem is undecidable—infinity sets a fundamental boundary on what computers can know.

punchline.text: Even computers hit a wall when infinity gets involved.

quiz.question: What is the halting problem?
options: It's impossible to determine if any program will eventually stop
Computers overheat when running too long
